step_by_step_thinking:
The anterior two-thirds of the tongue develop from the first pharyngeal arch, which includes contributions from both the maxillary process (lateral lingual swellings) and the median mandibular swelling. However, for the precise origin being asked about specifically related to these structures - namely the lateral lingual swellings and the tuberculum impar – it is well established that they contribute to the anterior part of this region. Hence, their contribution aligns with forming the majority of what becomes known as the anterior portion of the adult tongue.
